publishedAbstract: When previous mounts fail to release the below two articles will assist in releasing the previous volume mount for the new one to successfully mount. [Pod fails with: 'MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume " ": stat / /pods/ /volumes/kubernetes.io/: no such file or directory'](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/4220761) [RWO PVC fails to Mount with the Error "Multi-Attach error" or "is still being used"](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7000129) In the event the above two articles do not resolve the issue and get the pod to schedule, this most likely is a scenario such as presented with the Image Registry resources. The `config/cluster` also known as `configs.imageregistry.operator.openshift.io` does not allow enough time for the `image-registry` deployments to remain down after scaling to `--replicas=0`. This is due to the `image-registry` deployment being a managed resource of the `configs.imageregistry.operator.openshift.io`. This management bounces the deployment immediately back up, therefore not allowing enough time to release the old mount resulting in still experiencing the following error: ~~~ $ oc get pods-n openshift-image-registry NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE cluster-image-registry-operator-7f4dcd6b7f-2425v 1/1 Running 0 3d23h image-registry-b68667f95-79lkh 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 4h25m image-registry-b68667f95-hchdb 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 4h25m image-registry-b68667f95-lcl25 1/1 Running 0 4h25m node-ca-8rxr6 1/1 Running 1 4d2h node-ca-dzrz6 1/1 Running 1 4d2h node-ca-fzjmg 1/1 Running 1 4d2h node-ca-gpnx9 1/1 Running 1 4d2h node-ca-r8n52 1/1 Running 1 4d2h node-ca-z74sr 1/1 Running 1 4d2h Warning FailedMount 4m52s (x129 over 4h25m) kubelet MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume "pvc-92c85e4b-b33b-4401-ab84-aef5ed02a67f": rpc error: code = Internal desc = failed to try to restore FUSE mounts: stat /var/lib/kubelet/plugins/kubernetes.io/csi/openshift-storage.cephfs.csi.ceph.com/cbe53e43c8cccfd55260a4463e44e3faa4d11a4a9c60036695c335f5401b9ced/globalmount: no such file or directory ~~~
publishedTitle: Image Registry Pods Cannot Mount due to Stale Volume Mount "MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume pvc-xxx-xxx-xxx-xxx no such file or directory"
